scenario_results.reason: Produced an in-scope facilities execution package with explicit lifecycle-state progression, completion evidence, and bounded return-to-service posture.
Returned explicit prerequisite blockers for missing jurisdiction, permit, inspection, and tenant criteria records and did not proceed with deterministic dispositioning.
Preserved hold or rework posture while site-log, isolation, subcontractor, and CMMS evidence remained incomplete.
Identified the scope mismatch and open change-order dependency and routed the work into rework or escalation rather than silently advancing it.
Refused closure without traceable completion proof and returned a clear missing-evidence set for inspection, testing, and restart artifacts.
Maintained blocked progression because permit, outage, and site-readiness prerequisites were incomplete and tied the hold to authoritative records.
Maintained blocked completion while inspection, deficiency, O&M, warranty, and balancing artifacts remained unresolved.
Detected code-interpretation and licensed-trade overreach and routed the request to the proper authority.
Reversed the provisional progression when new safety and inspection blockers appeared and escalated instead of overriding them.
Preserved boundary discipline by routing change-order approval, AHJ response, and subcontractor scope acceptance outside the lane.
Escalated the conflicting public-baseline and tenant-policy closeout requirement without unsupported synthesis.
Adapted execution packaging to tenant CMMS codes and outage controls while preserving permit, inspection, safety, and approval guardrails.

notes: All 12 defined scenarios are represented in functionality_map.json scenario_coverage.
All required shared, execution-processing-and-fulfillment, and construction-and-field-services abilities are mapped and validated.
validated standard validation logic is met with full coverage of 12 facilities work-order execution scenarios.
Runtime remains specification-based and no final permit, code, trade, budget, staffing, or return-to-service approval authority is claimed.
